frameworks.name: feedforward evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Sensory cortex encodes evidence accumulated forward toward motor regions encoding action plan.

frameworks.name: feedback / recurrent cortical processing
frameworks.one_line: Choice-dependent feedback signals propagate from downstream regions back to early sensory cortex.

frameworks.name: frequency-specific cortical oscillations
frameworks.one_line: Narrow-band ~10 Hz oscillations interpreted as carriers of decision-related feedback.

core_question: Can decision-related feedforward and feedback signals in human cortex be dissociated during perceptual decision-making?
key_assumptions: Choice-predictive signals can be compared to sensory-evidence weighting on choice
MEG can resolve direction-of-flow signatures across cortical hierarchy
Feedforward and feedback components are additively separable in cortical activity
manipulations: Perceptual decision task with measurable sensory evidence and choice
Comparison of signals across cortical processing stages (early sensory through motor)
key_findings: Choice-dependent feedback signals were prominent in primary visual cortex
Feedback was expressed in a narrow band around 10 Hz
Feedback signals built up during decision formation, following downstream choice-predictive dynamics
interpretations: Pure feedforward accounts of perceptual decision-making are insufficient
Frequency-specific cortical oscillations carry computational role of decision feedback
